content.problemStatement:
Robert is a maths teacher and he made a question for his students.
Question is a matrix of NNN X NNN with all distinct elements from  111 to N∗NN*NN∗N. One has to apply **maxpool** function of filter matrix 222 X 222 to get the answer, that is for each 222 X 222 matrix you have to write the maximum element in corresponding matrix of size (N−1)(N-1)(N−1) X (N−1)(N-1)(N−1).

for example
if NNN = 333 and matrix given is

`1 7 4
2 3 5
9 8 6
`then after applying maxpool function we get:

`7 7
9 8
`Robert is a careless person and forgot the actual question or NNN X NNN matrix but he knows the answer or (N−1)(N-1)(N−1) X (N−1)(N-1)(N−1) matrix.

Can you help him to derive any valid question matrix(must contain all the numbers from 111 to NNN * NNN ) back from the answer given?

Inputs are given in such a way that the answer always exists. In case of multiple answers print any.

###Input


The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN:- The size of the question matrix or matrix before the filter is applied.
Next (N−1)(N-1)(N−1) lines contains (N−1)(N-1)(N−1) space-seperated integers in each line.
constraints: 1≤A[i][j]≤(N∗N)1 \leq A[i][j] \leq (N*N) 1≤A[i][j]≤(N∗N) , for all 1≤i,j≤(N−1)1 \leq i,j  \leq  (N-1) 1≤i,j≤(N−1), and AAA is the matrix after the filter is applied.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the matrix of size NNN X NNN . Print NNN lines where each line will contain NNN integers separated by a space.
